### Query Summary
The user is asking for the specific date when a local English newspaper featured a sculpture and war memorial located in Forbury Gardens. This query requires identifying the specific newspaper, verifying its location and content, and retrieving historical publication data regarding its name change.

### Necessary Skills and Tools

**1. Ground_Google_Search_Tool**
*   **How it helps:** This tool is ideal for initial discovery. It can search for the specific phrase "sculpture and war memorial in Forbury gardens" to identify the newspaper. Once the newspaper is identified, the tool can search for its history or name changes, providing real-time, verifiable information with citations.
*   **Best Practice:** Use this first to confirm the existence of the memorial and the specific newspaper associated with it.

**2. Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool**
*   **How it helps:** This tool is highly effective for retrieving specific historical facts about the newspaper. It can search Wikipedia for the newspaper's name change history, providing structured data, dates, and context that general web searches might miss. It is particularly useful for verifying the "local newspaper" aspect and the specific event of the name change.
*   **Best Practice:** Use this to cross-reference the date found in Google with Wikipedia for accuracy, as Wikipedia often has detailed records of historical publications.

**3. Generalist_Solution_Generator_Tool**
*   **How it helps:** This tool can act as a starting point for a step-by-step reasoning process. It can help break down the complex query into subtasks (e.g., "Find the newspaper," "Find the name change date") and generate a preliminary response.
*   **Best Practice:** Use this to outline the research steps before diving into specialized searches, ensuring the query is addressed logically.

### Additional Considerations
*   **Verification:** Since the Generalist_Solution_Generator_Tool may provide hallucinated information, the final answer must be verified using the Ground_Google_Search_Tool or Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool.
*   **Specificity:** The query relies on specific details (Forbury Gardens, sculpture, war memorial). The tools should be used with precise queries to avoid generic results.
*   **Multi-step Reasoning:** The query involves identifying a location, finding a specific entity (newspaper), and retrieving a historical fact (name change date). A multi-step approach using the specialized tools is recommended over a single generalist query.

In its final decades, it changed names multiple times: it was known as the *Berkshire Mercury* from July 11, 1970, to December 24, 1980; then as *The Mercury* from December 13, 1980, to June 30, 1983; and finally as the *Reading and Berkshire Mercury* from July 6, 1983, until its cessation on May 28, 1987[5](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reading_Mercury). The *Reading Evening Post* was launched on September 14, 1965[1](https://grokipedia.com/page/reading_post), [2](https://www.getreading.co.uk/news/reading-berkshire-news/reading-post-heart-town-forefront-8300799). It later rebranded as the *Reading Post* and switched from daily to weekly publication in May 2009[1](https://grokipedia.com/page/reading_post), [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reading_Post), [2](https://www.getreading.co.uk/news/reading-berkshire-news/reading-post-heart-town-forefront-8300799), [4](https://www.holdthefrontpage.co.uk/2014/news/sounding-of-last-post-lamented-by-staff/). The final print edition of the *Reading Post* was published on December 17, 2014[1](https://grokipedia.com/page/reading_post), [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reading_Post), [4](https://www.holdthefrontpage.co.uk/2014/news/sounding-of-last-post-lamented-by-staff/). After this, it transitioned to a digital-only format under the name Get Reading, which was later rebranded as BerkshireLive in 2019, before ultimately ceasing operations in November 2023[1](https://grokipedia.com/page/reading_post), [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reading_Post).

The *Reading Chronicle* has a much older lineage, beginning its life as the *Berkshire Chronicle* in 1825[5](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reading_Chronicle), [6](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEbEjrXm59VmokfP7Pc8ysyAbf2egRhNylzLQC8mVvWRlqhZlmVanxmHubDK-kxHHIY1r9vZlKotFDBq-YtQHtepmYjR9vG7flD2xVUVQCo_9_ZVStVC2kh-_ay-o5HTe8LHV9_Wq8HhCobumPC1C7Lnrjw83U3kaMzKUrqjpIpoi2uWKSjIDZ2xaDf-ovohe9u6_pWr3WmpJ-uqMtWfRY=), [7](https://www.findmypast.ie/newspapers/england/berkshire-chronicle). Between 1912 and 1965, it underwent name changes, being known as the *Berkshire Daily*, then the *Berkshire Chronicle*, and then the *Reading and Berkshire Chronicle*[8](https://www.readingchronicle.co.uk/news/13405465.chronicle-is-back-in-the-heart-of-reading/), [6](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEbEjrXm59VmokfP7Pc8ysyAbf2egRhNylzLQC8mVvWRlqhZlmVanxmHubDK-kxHHIY1r9vZlKotFDBq-YtQHtepmYjR9vG7flD2xVUVQCo_9_ZVStVC2kh-_ay-o5HTe8LHV9_Wq8HhCobumPC1C7Lnrjw83U3kaMzKUrqjpIpoi2uWKSjIDZ2xaDf-ovohe9u6_pWr3WmpJ-uqMtWfRY=). In 1965, it was renamed the *Reading Chronicle*, a name it continues to use today[8](https://www.readingchronicle.co.uk/news/13405465.chronicle-is-back-in-the-heart-of-reading/). The *Reading Chronicle* is a weekly newspaper[5](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reading_Chronicle).

The English local newspaper, the Reading Evening Post, did not change its name to the Reading Chronicle. These are two separate and distinct publications.

The **Reading Evening Post** was launched on September 14, 1965[1](https://grokipedia.com/page/reading_post), [2](https://www.getreading.co.uk/news/local-news/50-years-reading-evening-post-10044713). In May 2009, it dropped "Evening" from its name and became known as the Reading Post[1](https://grokipedia.com/page/reading_post), [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reading_Post). The print edition of the Reading Post ceased publication in December 2014, and it then transitioned to an online-only format under the name Get Reading. This digital operation was later rebranded as Berkshire Live in 2019[1](https://grokipedia.com/page/reading_post), [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reading_Post).
